root.name: Chemical Disciplines
root.description: The chemical sciences organized by discipline — from organic, inorganic and physical chemistry through analytical, materials, electro-, photo- and computational chemistry to the nuclear, environmental, medicinal and supramolecular frontiers. A deep authoritative map of chemistry's branches, their core theory, and their active research fronts.

pillars.name: Organic Chemistry
pillars.scope: structure, reactivity, synthesis and mechanisms of carbon compounds; functional groups, stereochemistry, pericyclic and radical chemistry, total synthesis, organometallics in synthesis

pillars.name: Inorganic Chemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry of all the elements beyond simple carbon; coordination compounds, main-group and transition-metal chemistry, bioinorganic, solid-state and cluster chemistry, ligand field theory

pillars.name: Physical Chemistry
pillars.scope: the physical principles underlying chemical systems; thermodynamics, chemical kinetics, quantum chemistry, statistical mechanics, spectroscopy, surface and colloid science

pillars.name: Analytical Chemistry
pillars.scope: identification, separation and quantification of matter; chromatography, mass spectrometry, spectroscopic and electroanalytical methods, chemometrics, sensors

pillars.name: Biochemistry
pillars.scope: the chemistry of living systems; proteins and enzymes, nucleic acids, metabolism and bioenergetics, lipids and membranes, structural and chemical biology

pillars.name: Theoretical & Computational Chemistry
pillars.scope: prediction and modeling of chemical structure and behavior; electronic-structure methods (DFT, ab initio), molecular dynamics, force fields, cheminformatics, machine-learned potentials

pillars.name: Materials Chemistry
pillars.scope: design and synthesis of functional solids and soft matter; ceramics, semiconductors, catalysts, framework materials (MOFs/COFs), energy-storage and 2D materials

pillars.name: Polymer Chemistry
pillars.scope: synthesis, characterization and physics of macromolecules; chain-growth and step-growth polymerization, controlled/living methods, polymer physics, biopolymers

pillars.name: Electrochemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry of electron transfer at interfaces; voltammetry, batteries and fuel cells, electrocatalysis, corrosion, bioelectrochemistry

pillars.name: Photochemistry & Radiation Chemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry driven by light and ionizing radiation; excited states, photocatalysis, luminescence, solar energy conversion, radiolysis

pillars.name: Nuclear & Radiochemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry of radioactive nuclei and nuclear transformations; isotope production, actinide chemistry, nuclear fuel cycle, radiopharmaceuticals, radioanalytics

pillars.name: Environmental & Green Chemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry of the natural environment and sustainable design; atmospheric and aquatic chemistry, pollutant fate, catalysis for sustainability, benign solvents, atom economy

pillars.name: Medicinal & Pharmaceutical Chemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry of drug discovery and development; structure-activity relationships, lead optimization, pharmacophores, ADME, chemical biology of therapeutics

pillars.name: Supramolecular Chemistry & Nanochemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry beyond the molecule; molecular recognition, self-assembly, host-guest systems, molecular machines, nanoparticles and nanostructured matter

pillars.name: Astrochemistry & Cosmochemistry
pillars.scope: chemistry of the cosmos; interstellar and circumstellar molecules, planetary and meteoritic chemistry, prebiotic chemistry, isotopic cosmochemistry
